name: dali-preview
description: Live UI preview for Tizen DALi applications. Preview your dali-ui C++ code in real-time within VS Code.

walkthroughs.title: Get started with DALi Preview
walkthroughs.description: Set up your runtime (Docker or Native) and render your first DALi preview in 5 minutes.

steps.title: Welcome
steps.description:
Quick tour of how DALi Preview works, plus the two runtime options.
[Open Documentation](command:dali.showReadme)

steps.title: Install Docker
steps.description:
Pre-fills the install command in a terminal — sudo password once. Grants this session access immediately (setfacl) — **no reboot or reload needed**.
[Install via Terminal](command:dali.installDocker)
[Verify Docker Access](command:dali.verifyDocker)

steps.title: Download DALi Runtime Image
steps.description:
Pulls the ~290 MB runtime image from GHCR with a progress bar. Verify Docker auto-prompts this — or run it directly here. Subsequent previews use the cached image, so this happens only once per version.
[Download Runtime Image](command:dali.pullRuntimeImage)

steps.title: Explore the Samples
steps.description:
Final step. Copies a guided **Samples** tour into a folder you pick and opens it in a new window — start at its `README.md`, then open any `*.preview.dali.cpp` and hit Ctrl+S to render (~1 second, image already cached).
[Open Samples](command:dali.openExamples)

enumDescriptions: Render inside the containerized DALi runtime — no host DALi install needed.
Compile against a host-installed DALi prefix and run under Xvfb — for uifw developers who rebuild DALi itself and want previews to reflect their fresh .so files.
daliPreview.runtimeMode.description: Where preview builds run. 'docker' (default) uses the containerized DALi runtime. 'local' compiles against the host DALi install at daliPreview.daliPrefix — switch to it via the 'DALi Preview: Use Local DALi Runtime' command, which also picks the folder.

daliPreview.daliPrefix.description: Path to the local DALi install prefix (the directory containing lib/ and include/). Used only when runtimeMode is 'local'. Leave empty to auto-detect from $DESKTOP_PREFIX, a workspace setenv file, or ~/dali-env/opt.

daliPreview.dockerImage.description: Container image used when runtimeMode is 'docker'. Tags are appended automatically (e.g. ':dali_2.5.18'). Leave empty to auto-detect: on the Samsung corporate network the BART GHCR proxy (ghcr-docker-remote.bart.sec.samsung.net/lwc0917/dali-preview-runtime) is used to avoid intermittent GHCR pull drops; otherwise ghcr.io/lwc0917/dali-preview-runtime. Set explicitly to pin a registry.

daliPreview.daliVersionTag.description: Image tag selecting the DALi version. Use 'latest' to follow the rolling tag, or pin a specific tag (e.g. 'tizen_10.0_release').

daliPreview.runtimeUpdatePolicy.description: How to handle a newer DALi runtime image in the registry (checked once per day in docker mode, via digest comparison). 'off': never check. 'notify' (default): show a notification + status-bar badge when an update is available — you click to apply it. 'auto': download and apply the update automatically in the background.

daliPreview.extensionUpdatePolicy.description: How to handle a newer *extension* release on GitHub. Because DALi Preview is installed from a .vsix (not the Marketplace), VS Code never auto-updates it — this check fills that gap (once per day, via github.com so no API rate-limit). 'off': never check. 'notify' (default): show a notification when a newer release is available; clicking 'Update now' re-runs the installer in a terminal. (No 'auto' — a running extension can't hot-swap itself.)

daliPreview.previewWidth.description: Preview canvas width in pixels. Defaults to the TV FHD profile (1920×1080) — the target device for DALi UI apps. Override per file with // @preview-config: width=…

daliPreview.previewHeight.description: Preview canvas height in pixels. Defaults to the TV FHD profile (1920×1080).

daliPreview.background.description: Background style for the preview panel.

daliPreview.livePreview.description: Enable live preview: automatically re-render as you type using a debounce interval.

daliPreview.livePreviewDebounce.description: Debounce interval in milliseconds for live preview. 0 = update on every keystroke (best with the parser fast path, ~100ms per update). Raise this if you're on the slow harness fallback path or want to coalesce multiple rapid edits.

daliPreview.fontDirectories.description: Directories containing custom TTF/OTF font files. Fonts in these directories are registered via FontClient::AddCustomFontDirectory and can be referenced by filename in @preview-config (e.g. font=NotoSansKR.ttf).

daliPreview.logLevel.description: Log verbosity level for the DALi Preview output channel. Use 'trace' for AI-friendly structured JSON diagnostics.

daliPreview.disablePreviewServer.description: Diagnostic-only. When true, skip the long-running preview-server / dlopen / parser fast paths and always run the full g++ harness compile (~1100ms). Useful for measuring the slow path.

scripts.verify: npm run compile && npm run test:unit:no-coverage && npm run test:e2e
scripts.hooks:install: git config core.hooksPath .githooks && echo 'pre-push hook enabled (.githooks)'
scripts.test:e2e:all: npm run test:e2e && npm run test:e2e:server && npm run test:e2e:multifile
scripts.test:release: npm run test:unit && npm run test:click-to-code && npm run test:e2e:all
